description_long: This is a pair of old, weathered metal-framed chairs with wooden slats for the seat and backrest, mounted on square tiled platforms. The wood is painted green but is heavily chipped and worn, revealing the underlying material and giving a distressed appearance. One chair features a right-side armrest, while the other does not, and both have visible rust and corrosion on the metal parts. The platforms beneath the chairs are stained, with one showing dark, reddish marks suggestive of grime or possibly dried blood, adding a gritty, urban feel. Stickers and graffiti are present on the backrests, further emphasizing their placement in a neglected or vandalized public space. The overall look suggests these chairs are found in harsh outdoor environments such as abandoned bus stops, derelict parks, or urban alleyways.
description: A pair of distressed, green-painted wooden chairs with metal frames, mounted on stained tiled platforms and marked by graffiti and heavy wear. The design and condition evoke a gritty, urban outdoor setting.
description_view_0: Front view showing both chairs side by side, highlighting the chipped green paint, graffiti, and the presence of an armrest on the right chair.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the thickness of the wooden slats, the rusted metal frame, and the single armrest on the right chair.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of both chairs, the graffiti, and the full extent of the weathered wood and metal corrosion.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the absence of an armrest on the left chair and the stained tiled platforms beneath.
